content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby(). Since our sandboxes turned out to be separate copies (my file never showed your with_counts edits), I implemented case_sensitive against the original base sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, factored the core logic into a new helper _group_by(value, expr, case_sensitive) that both sync/async call. Signature: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False). When case_sensitive=False (default), sorting/grouping key uses ignore_case(expr(item)), but the emitted grouper preserves original case from the first item in each group. All 837 existing tests still pass. If you do end up merging our two diffs by hand, the with_counts branch and my case_sensitive branch are independent axes (one controls key normalization, the other controls output tuple shape) so they should compose by adding both params to the signature and applying with_counts on top of _group_by's output tuples.
